title: Tapeinidium flavicans
taxonomy: Tapeinidium flavicans is a species of fern in the family Lomariopsidaceae. It is native to tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The classification of Tapeinidium flavicans is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Lomariopsidaceae. The family Lomariopsidaceae is a group of ferns that are characterized by their simple or compound leaves and often woody rhizomes. This fern species is often found growing on trees and other vegetation in its native habitats.

intro: Tapeinidium flavicans, also known as the Yellow Tapeinidium, is a species of flowering plant in the fern family, Osmundaceae. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Tapeinidium flavicans include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
